title: Comparative DSA capstone

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Comparative DSA capstone; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Comparative DSA capstone
sources.claim: A DSA capstone specifies behavior and constraints, justifies structure and algorithm choice, proves key invariants, tests adversarial cases, and measures complexity.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A DSA capstone specifies behavior and constraints, justifies structure and algorithm choice, proves key invariants, tests adversarial cases, and measures complexity.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A route planner documents graph representation, shortest-path assumptions, tests disconnected and large graphs, and reports complexity. Your goal is to deliver, test and explain a selected-language solution with correctness argument, complexity analysis and alternative comparison. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Greedy, backtracking and dynamic programming; Directed and weighted graph algorithms; Sequence and range problem patterns; Language-switch diagnostic; Adversarial and property tests; All-pairs path reasoning; Intervals and sweep ordering. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: A DSA capstone specifies behavior and constraints, justifies structure and algorithm choice, proves key invariants, tests adversarial cases, and measures complexity. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Concept evidence is shared across C, C++, Java, and Python, but C pointer/lifetime safety, C++ iterator/ownership rules, Java generics/equality, and Python mutability/hashability are separate implementation facets that require evidence.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: A working demo can hide scale failure, language-specific undefined behavior, weak evidence, or an unjustified copied solution. The draft therefore rejects: Passing the visible examples is sufficient evidence for a portfolio DSA project.

includes: Solve an integrated problem with multiple candidate structures or strategies and defend the final choice.
Deliver, test and explain a selected-language solution with correctness argument, complexity analysis and alternative comparison.

Concept evidence is shared across C, C++, Java, and Python, but C pointer/lifetime safety, C++ iterator/ownership rules, Java generics/equality, and Python mutability/hashability are separate implementation facets that require evidence.
excludes: A working demo can hide scale failure, language-specific undefined behavior, weak evidence, or an unjustified copied solution.

outcomes: Deliver, test and explain a selected-language solution with correctness argument, complexity analysis and alternative comparison.

examples.title: Comparative DSA capstone: implementation transfer
examples.situation: Concept evidence is shared across C, C++, Java, and Python, but C pointer/lifetime safety, C++ iterator/ownership rules, Java generics/equality, and Python mutability/hashability are separate implementation facets that require evidence.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: Concept evidence is shared across C, C++, Java, and Python, but C pointer/lifetime safety, C++ iterator/ownership rules, Java generics/equality, and Python mutability/hashability are separate implementation facets that require evidence.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: A working demo can hide scale failure, language-specific undefined behavior, weak evidence, or an unjustified copied solution.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: Passing the visible examples is sufficient evidence for a portfolio DSA project.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Comparative DSA capstone, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
A working demo can hide scale failure, language-specific undefined behavior, weak evidence, or an unjustified copied solution.
